subject: Add the INFO.yaml
commit_message:
Add the INFO.yaml

I believed it should have been there (self service repo creation procedure)

Issue-ID: INT-1698

Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>
Change-Id: I96055526020f27524b342a3af30a84003161be25

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Looks OK to me
Patch Set 1: Verified+1 Code-Review+2
Removed Code-Review+2 by Morgan Richomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

Patch Set 1: Verified+1 Code-Review+2

(1 comment)
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 1:

I'm confused:

- this change is already rebased on origin/master
- this change has positive review and verification scores

but all I've got in Gerrit UI is "Rebase" button (which also shows change "is up to date with the target branch") and no "Submit" option 😞

I may try pushing this change to directly refs/heads/master but I'm not sure if that's what we're aiming at

Patch Set 1:

I suspect we might be in a need for a 'verified' flag from Jenkins hence added info yaml verify job https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/111878

Once it get merged will trigger it here to verify my assumption.

Jessica - was there recently any policy change in LF that disallows submitting only manually verified INFO.yaml changes?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I suspect we might be in a need for a 'verified' flag from Jenkins hence added info yaml verify job https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/111878
> 
> Once it get merged will trigger it here to verify my assumption.
> 
> Jessica - was there recently any policy change in LF that disallows submitting only manually verified INFO.yaml changes?

Jenkins hasn't verified the change, that's why we cannot merge it. The team will need to add the Jenkins Info verify job so that Jenkins can +1

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I suspect we might be in a need for a 'verified' flag from Jenkins hence added info yaml verify job https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/111878
> 
> Once it get merged will trigger it here to verify my assumption.
> 
> Jessica - was there recently any policy change in LF that disallows submitting only manually verified INFO.yaml changes?

Also... as a small reminder, we are not allowing manual +1 verify of INFO.yaml files going forward to allow for a full self serve repo creation in gerrit.linuxfoundation.org
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/PTL+2020-08-10

Patch Set 1:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/111878 is merged now
After the merge job completes, we can run recheck/reverify here

Patch Set 1:

recheck

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

One last thing. Self serve does add this file. It was just abandoned by the team:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/terraform/+/111455

I think we need to change the commit message since it currently reflects bad on the self serve process

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I still have the problem to create a change reference.
> Is there something remained to set up for this repo?
> 
> =======================
> git push origin HEAD:refs/master
> Enumerating objects: 71, done.
> Counting objects: 100% (71/71), done.
> Delta compression using up to 8 threads
> Compressing objects: 100% (63/63), done.
> Writing objects: 100% (69/69), 13.46 KiB | 1.92 MiB/s, done.
> Total 69 (delta 12), reused 0 (delta 0)
> remote: Resolving deltas: 100% (12/12)
> remote: error: branch refs/master:
> remote: You need 'Create' rights to create new references.
> remote: User: YoonsoonJahng
> remote: Contact an administrator to fix the permissions
> remote: Processing changes: refs: 1, done
> To ssh://gerrit.onap.org:29418/integration/terraform
>  ! [remote rejected] HEAD -> refs/master (prohibited by Gerrit: not permitted: create)
> error: failed to push some refs to 'ssh://YoonsoonJahng@gerrit.onap.org:29418/integration/terraform'

You have a typo in the git reference name, it's "refs/for/master", not "refs/master", so the complete push command should look like:

git push origin HEAD:refs/for/master
